name: Cashew Nut Brittle
recipeIngredient: Raw cashew nuts, unsalted, coarsely chopped, 3 cups
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter the sides of a 3-quart saucepan and add sugar, corn syrup, butter, and water.
2. Stir over low heat until the butter has melted.
3. Increase the heat and bring the mixture to a boil.
4. Continue cooking over moderate heat, without stirring, for about 20 minutes, until the temperature reaches 275°F (soft-crack stage) on a candy thermometer.
5. Add cashew nuts and continue cooking for about 5 minutes, until the candy thermometer registers 295°F (hard crack stage).
6. Remove from heat and immediately sift baking soda evenly over the surface. Be careful—the syrup will foam up.
7. Add vanilla and stir to distribute throughout the mixture.
8. Pour onto prepared baking sheets. Tilt sheets to spread about 1/2-inch thick.
9. Allow to cool until firm. Break into pieces and store in an airtight container.
